images.alt:
Alt text:

Ranked list titled MVP, Most Valuable Player, highest total Net Points, best overall. All ten players are from the New York Knicks. Karl-Anthony Towns is ranked first with a trophy icon at +79.7. OG Anunoby is second at +76.6. Jalen Brunson is third at +66.7. Mikal Bridges is fourth at +34.8. Josh Hart is fifth at +20.1. Jose Alvarado is sixth at +10.1. Landry Shamet is seventh at +7.8. Jordan Clarkson is eighth at +5.2. Miles McBride is ninth at +3.0. Mitchell Robinson is tenth at -12.2.

items.title: The De'Aaron Fox discourse is unfortunate.
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
The De'Aaron Fox discourse is unfortunate. 

First two rounds, Fox averaged 18.8 points on 46.2% FGs, shot 34.6% 3s. Spurs went 8-3 in those games.

Fox misses first 2 games of WCF with ankle injury that we all saw, then comes back and shot 35.3% FGs, 24% 3s.

items.title: You know the moment during end of games free throws when the teams bring in all their centers to defensive rebound the opponent knows a timeout is coming so they put their bigs in and everyone crashes?
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
You know the moment during end of games free throws when the teams bring in all their centers to defensive rebound the opponent knows a timeout is coming so they put their bigs in and everyone crashes?

Time permitting, why not try to run in transition and if nothing there after 4 secs call timeout?

items.title: Karl-Anthony Towns is the first and only player in NBA history to average more than 15 PPG while shooting:
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
Karl-Anthony Towns is the first and only player in NBA history to average more than 15 PPG while shooting:
above 55% from the field, 
above 45% from downtown and 
above 90% from the free-throw line 

across a full postseason (minimum > 5 games played)

items.title: well look
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
well look

In the 24 minutes Castle and Harper were on the floor together in 4th quarters, Spurs were outscored by 26 points and missed 26 of 35 shots and had a 4:13 AST-TO ratio.

In the 9 minutes Castle/Harper shared 4th quarter minutes WITHOUT Fox, Spurs were 2/11 FGs and outscored by 21 points.

items.title: I'll echo what some others have said:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
I'll echo what some others have said:

Boston & Milwaukee have largely moved quietly when they've made recent moves. They don't negotiate through the media. They keep things tight.

Now we're getting hourly updates on trade talks? Feels weird. Maybe it's a third team leaking. Just off for BOS & MIL.

Julian Champagnie has 11 points and 59 percent. Keldon Johnson has 4.4 points and 41.5 percent. Carter Bryant has 1.8 points and 76.5 percent. Luke Kornet has 0.6 points and 39.9 percent. Harrison Barnes has 0 points and 0 percent.

my god, I'm soooooooooooooo happy we don't have to spend the next three days debating if Wemby deserves to get suspended or if there would be an asterisk on the Knicks title if they won Game 6 etc...

items.title: Total Net Points | NBA Finals
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Total Net Points | NBA Finals 

How many points a player was estimated to add to their teams scoring margin in the series 

1️⃣ +19.9  OG Anunoby
2️⃣ +14.2  Karl-Anthony Towns
3️⃣ +12.4  Victor Wembanyama
—————————————
😵 -17.6  De’Aaron Fox

Learn more: espnanalytics.com/net-pts-expl...

images.alt: Horizontal bar chart titled Knicks vs Spurs, Total Net Points, NBA Finals, Knicks Win Series 4-1, data from ESPN Analytics. Bars run from highest to lowest net points. OG Anunoby leads at +19.9, followed by Karl-Anthony Towns at +14.2, both Knicks. Victor Wembanyama is at +12.4 for the Spurs. Josh Hart is at +9.7 for the Knicks. Devin Vassell is at +7.7 for the Spurs. Jalen Brunson is at +7.6 for the Knicks. Dylan Harper is at +5.2 for the Spurs. Mikal Bridges is at +4.9 for the Knicks. Julian Champagnie is at +0.8 for the Spurs. Ariel Hukporti is at +0.7 for the Knicks. Jeremy Sochan is at -0.7 for the Spurs. Luke Kornet is at -2.9 for the Spurs. Jordan Clarkson is at -3.2 and Jose Alvarado at -3.5, both Knicks. Harrison Barnes is at -3.6, Carter Bryant at -4.0, and Keldon Johnson at -4.3, all Spurs. Stephon Castle is at -7.1 for the Spurs. Mitchell Robinson is at -11.0, Miles McBride at -11.5, and Landry Shamet at -13.6, all Knicks. De’Aaron Fox is at -17.6 for the Spurs.

items.title: Jalen Brunson's Finals MVP run started two years ago with a team-friendly contract, and ended with 45 points and a ring

items.title: I Lived To See This
items.author: Last Night In Basketball
items.body_excerpt: Seventeen years to the day before I was born, the New York Knicks won their first NBA championship. Three years and two days later, they won another. It took 53 years — 19,392 days, of which I was alive for 14,281 of them — for it to happen again. After waiting my entire life, I…

items.title: Jalen Brunson scored 29 points in the second half of Game 5 last night
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
Jalen Brunson scored 29 points in the second half of Game 5 last night 

That’s the MOST POINTS scored in the second half of any NBA Finals game played in the NBA’s modern era.

Brunson did it in a Finals-clinching win on the road to break a 53-year title drought for the Knicks

items.title: June 14, 1987: James Worthy with the hustle play, Magic Johnson with the slam during the Lakers' 106-93 win over Boston in Game 6 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 14, 1987: James Worthy with the hustle play, Magic Johnson with the slam during the Lakers' 106-93 win over Boston in Game 6 of the NBA Finals.

Worthy scored 22 PTS, Magic had 16 PTS/19 AST, and Kareem Abdul-Jabbar scored 32 PTS as the Lakers clinched the NBA Championship.

items.title: “At some point, the accumulation of winning trades and signings, firings and hirings, comebacks and blowouts, depth and adaptability, risks and restraints, is just the reality. And the reality is, these Knicks were Goliaths hiding in plain sight.”

items.title: Dylan Harper whet #Spurs fans' appetite for more with a team-high 25 points in Game 5.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Dylan Harper whet #Spurs fans' appetite for more with a team-high 25 points in Game 5.

"We have a star in the making," Devin Vassell said of the rookie guard.

But the other “Slash Brothers" ended the season on a down note.
www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

media.title: Dylan Harper came up big for Spurs in Game 5 while other guards struggled
media.description: The No. 2 overall pick had a team-high 25 points on a night De'Aaron Fox and Stephon Castle combined for just 13 points.

items.title: At long last, the Knicks' title drought is over.
items.author: Zach Kram
items.body_excerpt:
At long last, the Knicks' title drought is over. 

I wrote about Jalen Brunson's MVP-worthy finale, the unique way the Knicks built a championship roster, and what a disappointing end means for the rising Spurs.

media.title: How the Knicks won the NBA Finals over the Spurs: What we learned
media.description: The Knicks are NBA champions! Here's how they built their team to get there, and what the Finals outcome means for both them and the Spurs.
